title: dual-agent consensus orchestration
summary: Added a repo-owned dual-agent consensus workflow that snapshots shared context, alternates proposal and review rounds, and only runs implementation, verification, and deployment hooks after explicit agreement.
completed: Added the dual-agent consensus orchestration tool
Added an example config and workflow documentation
Added end-to-end tests for consensus success and no-consensus failure
Updated build and operating docs to include the optional consensus step
decisions_locked: The orchestrator is vendor-agnostic and relies on local wrappers instead of direct provider API calls
Shared context is snapshotted at run start so both agents reason from the same frozen files
Implementation, verification, and deployment hooks only run after explicit consensus
open_questions: Which local wrapper interface should be standardized first for real Codex and Claude CLI integration
Whether consensus proposals should be validated against a stricter schema per task type
Whether deployment hooks should require an additional human approval step in production use
next_steps: Add real Codex and Claude wrappers that satisfy the response contract
Decide whether to add task-specific proposal schemas
Use the consensus loop for ambiguous or high-risk implementation slices
resume_from: Start from project_memory/current_context.md. The repo now has a tested dual-agent consensus loop, but real provider wrappers and production deploy guardrails are still the next step.
